channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Team17 has laid off members of its marketing and communications department
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 3, 2026

1 Min Read

UK studio Team17 has made an undisclosed number of layoffs within its marketing and communications department. 

It's unclear how many people have been impacted, but at least two people made redundant by the Worms developer-publisher indicated the entire internal PR team has been jettisoned in posts on Bluesky and LinkedIn.

A statement provided to Game Developer by Team17 confirmed some roles have been eliminated.

"Following a review of our business operations, we have made changes to our marketing and communications structure. We would like to thank the colleagues affected for their contribution to Team17 and the support they have provided to our games, development partners and communities," said a spokesperson for the company. 

"While our structure has evolved, our commitment to supporting our games, development partners, players, media and creator communities remains unchanged. We continue to work closely with both internal teams and external partners to ensure our communications, creator engagement and media relations activities continue to support the success of our games and partners."

Related:Mina the Hollower sells 300,000 copies in three days

The company, which has also published notable titles such as Overcooked, Hell Let Loose, and Dredge, previously laid off a significant number of staff in 2023. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton.
